links.targetTitle: SwarmChannel implements IChannel so the reactor SyncManager is transport-agnostic
Connect acts as a bridge between the Switchboard ecosystem and the decentralized Swarm network.

global.title: Connect's Swarm integration is split into three layers: bee-reactor-adapter, SwarmChannel, and Connect UI
global.content:
The integration decomposes into three layers with clear seams:

**Layer 1 — `bee-reactor-adapter` (npm)**: the `SwarmClient` class wraps the Bee SDK. It exposes `uploadData`/`downloadData` for encrypted `/bytes`, `uploadFile`/`downloadFile` for ACT-protected `/bzz`, `createGrantees`/`patchGrantees` for ACT grantee lists, `pssSend`/`pssSubscribe` for 1-to-1 messaging, `gsocSend`/`gsocSubscribe` for sub-second notifications, and manifest helpers (`updateManifest`, `readManifest`, `updateDriveManifest`, `updateUserManifest`). It also handles per-topic write locks, 3-retry backoff on feed writes, and auto-detects reference vs inline feed format.

**Layer 2 — SwarmChannel**: a native `IChannel` implementation wired via `ReactorBuilder.withSync()` and a `CompositeChannelFactory` that routes `"swarm"` and `"gql"` config types to the right sub-factory. Owns push (outbox) and pull (inbox), cursor tracking via `sync_cursors`, and dead letters via `sync_dead_letters`.

**Layer 3 — Connect UI**: the `swarm-settings/` panel shows connection status, stamp health, doc tree with sync badges, and share/import controls; the `components/chat/` panel ships the chat UI.

The plugin layer still owns Bee detection, stamp selection, wallet key derivation, and UI event emission.
global.description: SwarmClient wraps the Bee SDK (CRUD + ACT + PSS + GSOC); SwarmChannel implements IChannel for reactor sync; swarm-settings and chat panels expose state and controls in Connect UI.
